metadata.title: Guilherme Passarinho | Frontend Developer
metadata.description: Frontend engineer building accessible, high-performance web experiences.

profile.tagline: I build fast, accessible, and user-centric digital experiences.

about.p1: I believe well-crafted software changes people's experience. That's why every project I develop — whether it's an interface built with React and Styled Components, state managed with Redux, or a backend orchestrated in Firebase — is designed with attention to detail and a focus on the end user. All of it written in TypeScript, from the first to the last file.
about.p2: I work primarily with the modern JavaScript ecosystem, but I navigate the full stack whenever needed.
about.p3: Outside of work, I recharge by watching movies, binge-watching series, or gaming with friends.

exp-1.description: Working on the development and maintenance of interfaces for the Goomer ecosystem, a digital menu and self-service platform for restaurants. I use React, Next.js, and Styled Components for UI, Redux and Saga for state management, and Axios for API integration — all in TypeScript.

exp-2.description: Worked on-site at Rabobank, a global Dutch bank focused on agribusiness credit, supporting the bank's ERP management. Used the Microsoft ecosystem — Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power BI — to develop solutions within the Power Platform and optimize internal processes.

exp-3.description: Worked on the backoffice development for the American pizza chain Domino's, contributing to the maintenance and evolution of the internal platform. Used React with Redux and Saga for state management and Bootstrap for building the interfaces.

exp-4.description: Worked as QA testing the Rodobank mobile application for Android and iOS, validating new features before release to end users.

proj-1.title: GoHoot
proj-1.description: Real-time interactive quiz platform. Create personalized quizzes and play with friends featuring live rankings, gamification, and multi-device support.

proj-2.title: Roque OS
proj-2.description: macOS-style web operating system. I contribute new features and built the iOS and Android app using Capacitor.
